topic.name: Student Loan Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative amendments to the Higher Education Act of 1965 regarding student loan borrowing limits, interest rates, loan consolidation, and borrower protections.

Document 1: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to limit the unpaid amount of Direct Stafford loans that students may amass before completing their undergraduate education.
Caps the annual interest rate for Direct Stafford loans at 6.8%.
Document 2: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to allow certain borrowers to consolidate their private education loans as Direct Consolidation Loans.
Directs the Secretary of Education to inform and educate students and their families about this loan consolidation program.
Document 3: This bill amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to require a student loan borrower's prepayment amount on a Federal Family Education Loan, Federal Direct Loan, or Federal Perkins Loan to be applied first toward outstanding fees.
It amends the Truth in Lending Act to require a borrower's prepayment amount on a private education loan to be applied first toward outstanding fees.
Document 4: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to limit the unpaid amount of Direct Stafford loans that students may amass before completing their undergraduate education.
Caps the annual interest rate for Direct Stafford loans at 6.8%.
Document 5: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to require that when the holder of a borrower's loans receives a prepayment for such loans, it is to be applied first toward any fees due.
Requires an institution of higher education holding a borrower's loans under the Federal Perkins Loan program to first apply any excess payments by the borrower toward any fees due.
Document 6: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to set the annual interest rate on Direct Loans at the bond equivalent rate on 91-day Treasury bills plus: 1.85% for Direct Stafford Loans.

Requires institutions of higher education to provide student borrowers of title IV loans, prior to or at the time of their departure from school, with information regarding their options for loan consolidation.
Document 7: This bill amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to expand lender disclosure requirements.
It revises and expands required elements of pre-loan counseling to include a borrower's estimated debt-to-income ratio at graduation.

topic.name: Collective Bargaining Representation
topic.description: Legislative amendments focusing on the procedures and rights surrounding collective bargaining representation and elections under the National Labor Relations Act.

Document 1: Amends the National Labor Relations Act (NLRA) to make it an unlawful labor practice for a labor organization or its agents to interfere with the rights of employees to organize and select representation to collectively bargain.
Requires the NLRB to give 14 days advance notice before a hearing when it is investigating an election petition.
Document 2: Amends the National Labor Relations Act (NLRA) to make it an unlawful labor practice for a labor organization or its agents to interfere with the rights of employees to organize and select representation to bargain collectively.
Prohibits an election less than 40 calendar days following the filing of an election petition.
Document 3: Requires the NLRB to require that no investigative hearing take place until at least 14 days after the filing of an election petition regarding collective bargaining representation.

Requires the NLRB, in cases where it finds that a question of representation exists, to direct an election by secret ballot as soon as practicable but not before 35 calendar days after the filing of the election petition.
Document 4: Requires the NLRB to require that no investigative hearing take place until at least 14 days after the filing of an election petition regarding collective bargaining representation.

Requires the NLRB, in cases where it finds that a question of representation exists, to direct an election by secret ballot as soon as practicable but not before 35 calendar days after the filing of the election petition.
Document 5: Requires the Board also, not earlier than seven days after a final determination of the appropriate bargaining unit, to acquire from the employer a list of all employees eligible to vote in the election.
Requires the NLRB to direct an election by secret ballot as soon as practicable but not before 35 calendar days after the filing of the election petition.
Document 6: Requires the NLRB, in cases where it finds that a question of representation exists, to direct an election by secret ballot as soon as practicable but not before 35 calendar days after the filing of the election petition.
Requires such hearings to be non-adversarial, and the hearing officer, in collaboration with the parties, to identify any relevant and material pre-election issues.
Document 7: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to require the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) to decide, before the election of a labor organization as the exclusive collective bargaining representative of all employees of an appropriate unit.
Prohibits an election less than 40 calendar days following the filing of an election petition.

Document 3: Requires the Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) to issue regulations requiring that a specified warning label be placed on the packaging of any video game that is rated T (Teen) or higher.
Document 4: Prohibits the Bureau of Alcohol, Firearms, and Explosives (ATF) from purchasing, using, or distributing any hand tool or tool kit on which any reminder about forfeiture appears.
Document 5: This bill prohibits an air carrier operating an aircraft from charging ticketed passengers a fee for the use of a lavatory on such aircraft.
If an aircraft lacks an adequate number of functioning lavatories at the time of boarding, the carrier shall permit an alternative equivalent air transportation without charging an additional fee.
Document 6: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to deem a food to be misbranded if it contains genetically-engineered fish unless the food bears a label stating that fact.
Document 7: Prohibits an air carrier from charging a fee for four or fewer bags checked by a member of the Armed Forces traveling on official orders.

topic.name: Elder Abuse Prevention and Justice
topic.description: Legislation aimed at preventing, prosecuting, and providing assistance for elder abuse and exploitation.

conducting outreach activities and public awareness campaigns to ensure that abuse victims receive appropriate assistance.
Document 2: establish requirements for the Department of Justice (DOJ) with respect to investigating and prosecuting elder abuse crimes
combatting elder abuse requires support for victims and prevention.
Document 5: Directs the Attorney General to study and report to Congress on state laws and practices relating to elder abuse, neglect, and exploitation
Authorize appropriations for FY2009-FY2015.
Document 6: Establishes within the Department of Justice (DOJ) an Office of Elder Justice, which shall address issues relating to elder abuse
provide information, training, and technical assistance to assist states and local governments in preventing, investigating, prosecuting, and mitigating the impact of elder abuse.

topic.name: Tariff Suspension on Textiles and Footwear

Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States

uppers of vegetable fibers

topic.name: Mental Health and Workforce Development
topic.description: Legislation focused on improving mental health services and training healthcare professionals to address the needs of vulnerable populations, particularly in community settings.

Document 1: Amends the Public Health Service Act to require the Secretary of Health and Human Services to award grants for demonstration projects to provide coordinated and integrated services to individuals with mental illnesses.
Requires the Secretary to make funds available for the development or expansion of programs to provide integrated treatment services for individuals with a serious mental illness.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services to establish a program for the training of medical residents in community-based settings.
Requires the Secretary to give preference to entities that support teaching programs that address the health care needs of vulnerable populations.
Document 3: Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to establish a Geriatric and Gerontology Loan Repayment Program to enter into contracts with qualified physicians and other professionals to pay educational loans in exchange for providing services to older adults.
Establishes the National Advisory Council on the Geriatric and Gerontology Loan Repayment Program.
Document 5: Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to expand training for health professionals in the fields of geriatrics, long-term care, and chronic care management.
Provides for information to veterans about training, internship, and employment opportunities in geriatrics, gerontology, and long-term care.
Document 6: Amends the Public Health Service Act and the Social Security Act to expand the collection and analysis of data in programs of the Department of Health and Human Services.
HHS must engage in activities regarding health workforce diversity.
Document 7: Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to expand training for health professionals in the fields of geriatrics, long-term care, and chronic care management.
Provides for the development of information and referral protocols for family caregivers of Medicare beneficiaries.

topic.name: U.S. Immigration Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts related to immigration policies and procedures in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to authorize appropriations for the state criminal alien assistance program.
Document 2: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to make the special immigrant non-minister religious worker program permanent.
Document 3: Considers the period spent by an alien working as an interpreter or translator with the U.S. Armed Forces or federal agencies under the Chief of Mission in Afghanistan and Iraq as a period of residence and physical presence in the United States for naturalization purposes.
Document 4: Prohibits a consular officer from issuing an immigrant or nonimmigrant visa to any alien who is a citizen or national of a country designated by the World Health Organization as having widespread and intense transmissions.
Document 5: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to set forth provisions regarding the expedited removal of inadmissible arriving aliens.
Document 6: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to increase criminal penalties for certain removed aliens who illegally reenter the United States.
Document 7: Makes 3,000 immigrant visas available for individuals who were born in Tibet and have been continuously residing in India or Nepal.

topic.name: Food Safety Regulations
topic.description: The documents discuss various legislative measures aimed at improving food safety, reducing foodborne illnesses, and regulating harmful substances in food packaging.

Document 1: Amends the Agricultural Marketing Act of 1946 to direct the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to distribute a summary notice containing product information about each food or food product subject to such recall.
Defines 'Class I recall' as a food recall classification that covers a health-hazard situation.
Document 2: Prohibits the distribution of a food if its container is composed, in whole or in part, of bisphenol A (BPA) or can release BPA into food.
Requires the Secretary to promote and facilitate the use of BPA replacements.
Document 3: Establishes requirements that address food waste that occurs when people throw out fresh food because of their confusion over the meaning of expiration dates on food labels.
USDA and the Department of Health and Human Services must educate consumers on the meaning of quality date and safety date food labels.
Document 5: Amends the Federal Meat Inspection Act, the Poultry Products Inspection Act, and the Egg Products Inspection Act to include in the definition of 'adulterated' a product bearing or containing a pathogen or contaminant associated with serious illness or death.
Directs the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to identify significant foodborne disease pathogens.

topic.name: Firearm Regulations in D.C.
topic.description: The documents discuss legislative amendments aimed at regulating firearm ownership and usage in the District of Columbia, emphasizing the rights of individuals under federal law.

Document 1: Amends specified law prohibiting the killing of wild birds and wild animals in the District of Columbia to declare that nothing in it or any other provision of law shall authorize or be construed to permit the Council, the Mayor, or any governmental or regulatory authority of the District to prohibit, constructively prohibit, or unduly burden the ability of persons otherwise not prohibited from possessing firearms under federal law from acquiring, possessing in their homes or businesses, or using for sporting, self-protection or other lawful purposes, any firearm neither prohibited by federal law nor subject to the National Firearms Act.
Denies the District any authority to enact laws or regulations that discourage or eliminate the private ownership or use of firearms.
Document 2: Amends specified law prohibiting the killing of wild birds and wild animals in the District of Columbia to declare that nothing in it or any other provision of law shall authorize or be construed to permit the Council, the Mayor, or any governmental or regulatory authority of the District to prohibit, constructively prohibit, or unduly burden the ability of persons otherwise not prohibited from possessing firearms under federal law from acquiring, possessing in their homes or businesses, transporting for legitimate purposes, or using for sporting, self-protection or other lawful purposes, any firearm neither prohibited by federal law nor subject to the National Firearms Act.

Declares that nothing in such prohibitions shall be construed to prohibit the District from regulating the carrying of firearms by a person, either concealed or openly, other than at the person's dwelling place, place of business, or on other land possessed by the person.
Document 3: Amends specified law prohibiting the killing of wild birds and wild animals in the District of Columbia to declare that nothing in it or any other provision of law shall authorize or be construed to permit the Council, the Mayor, or any governmental or regulatory authority of the District to prohibit, constructively prohibit, or unduly burden the ability of persons otherwise not prohibited from possessing firearms under federal law from acquiring, possessing in their homes or businesses, transporting for legitimate purposes, or using for sporting, self-protection or other lawful purposes, any firearm neither prohibited by federal law nor subject to the National Firearms Act.
Denies the District any authority to enact laws or regulations that discourage or eliminate the private ownership or use of firearms.
Document 4: Amends specified law prohibiting the killing of wild birds and wild animals in the District of Columbia to declare that nothing in it or any other provision of law shall authorize or be construed to permit the Council, the Mayor, or any governmental or regulatory authority of the District to prohibit, constructively prohibit, or unduly burden the ability of persons otherwise not prohibited from possessing firearms under federal law from acquiring, possessing in their homes or businesses, transporting for legitimate purposes, or using for sporting, self-protection or other lawful purposes, any firearm neither prohibited by federal law nor subject to the National Firearms Act.

Declares that nothing in such prohibitions shall be construed to prohibit the District from regulating the carrying of firearms by a person, either concealed or openly, other than at the person's dwelling place, place of business, or on other land possessed by the person.
Document 5: Amends specified law prohibiting the killing of wild birds and wild animals in the District of Columbia to declare that nothing in it or any other provision of law shall authorize or be construed to permit the Council, the Mayor, or any governmental or regulatory authority of the District to prohibit, constructively prohibit, or unduly burden the ability of persons otherwise not prohibited from possessing firearms under federal law from acquiring, possessing in their homes or businesses, transporting for legitimate purposes, or using for sporting, self-protection or other lawful purposes, any firearm neither prohibited by federal law nor subject to the National Firearms Act.
Denies the District any authority to enact laws or regulations that discourage or eliminate the private ownership or use of firearms.
Document 6: Amends specified law prohibiting the killing of wild birds and wild animals in the District of Columbia to declare that nothing in it or any other provision of law shall authorize or be construed to permit the Council, the Mayor, or any governmental or regulatory authority of the District to prohibit, constructively prohibit, or unduly burden the ability of persons otherwise not prohibited from possessing firearms under federal law from acquiring, possessing in their homes or businesses, or using for sporting, self-protection or other lawful purposes, any firearm neither prohibited by federal law nor subject to the National Firearms Act.

Declares that nothing in such prohibitions shall be construed to prohibit the District from regulating or prohibiting the carrying of firearms by a person, either concealed or openly, other than at the person's dwelling place, place of business, or on other land possessed by the person.
Document 7: District of Columbia House Voting Rights Act of 2009 - (Sec. 2) Considers the District of Columbia a congressional district for purposes of representation in the House of Representatives.
Declares that the District shall not be considered a state for purposes of representation in the Senate.

topic.name: Education Funding and Accountability
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at ensuring equitable funding for schools and establishing accountability systems for educational performance.

Document 1: Amends part A of title I of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to condition local educational agency (LEA) receipt of school improvement funds on...
Requires states to provide the public with annual up-to-date school-by-school listings of per-pupil state and local spending.
Document 2: Amends part A of title I of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to condition local educational agency (LEA) receipt of school improvement funds on...
Requires annual LEA report cards to include certain information on state and local spending per pupil in schools.
Document 3: Amends the school improvement program under part A of title I of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to require each state to establish a multi-tiered accountability system...
Requires distinct tiers for schools: (1) making AYP; (2) nearly making AYP, achieving student academic growth...
Document 4: Amends part A of title I of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to allow states to measure student progress toward academic achievement standards...
Requires such states to adopt college and career ready academic content standards.
Document 5: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to exempt teachers who teach multiple subjects in certain small middle or secondary schools from being required to be highly qualified...
Allows states to use a growth model in calculating whether students are making adequately yearly progress (AYP)...
Document 6: Amends part A of title I of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to condition local educational agency (LEA) receipt of school improvement funds on...
Directs the Inspector General of the Department of Education...to audit 5 states and 10 LEAs to determine their progress in meeting these requirements.
Document 7: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to allow states to exclude the results of limited English proficient students...from adequate yearly progress (AYP) assessments...
Provides that, in determining AYP, a student in more than one group subject to measurable annual objectives shall be counted only toward one such group.

topic.name: U.S. Foreign Policy and Human Rights
topic.description: This cluster discusses U.S. legislative measures aimed at promoting human rights, democracy, and stability in various countries, particularly in the context of military and diplomatic strategies.

Document 1: Prohibits the federal government from providing the government of Vietnam with nonhumanitarian development, trade, economic, and security assistance during any fiscal year that exceeds the amount of such assistance provided during FY2009, unless such increases are matched or exceeded by additional funding for programs focusing on human rights and democracy promotion.

Authorizes the President to provide U.S. assistance through appropriate nongovernmental organizations and the Human Rights Defenders Fund for the support of individuals and organizations to promote human rights in Vietnam.
Document 2: Directs the President to condition U.S. political, military, and economic assistance to Iraq upon Iraq's meeting specified benchmarks.
States that it is U.S. policy to undertake a sustained diplomatic effort to engage the governments of regional nations on issues that impact the situation in Iraq.
Document 3: States that it is U.S. policy to support efforts by the people of Somalia to achieve peace, economic growth, and democracy, and to eradicate extremism and terrorism from their country and region.
Authorizes the President to provide assistance to support efforts for a peaceful resolution of the conflict in Somalia as well as the establishment of a representative government in Somalia.
Document 4: Declares that it is critical that a comprehensive security plan is developed for Iraq and the region that accepts the necessity for a deliberate U.S. redeployment of forces from Iraq.
A planned end to U.S. involvement in Iraq will serve to force Iraqi leaders to assume responsibility for security and governance.
Document 5: Directs the President to implement a regional diplomatic plan in conjunction with the U.N. Security Council to curtail the ongoing civil war in Iraq.
States that it is U.S. policy to support the goals specified in the International Compact for Iraq.
Document 6: Urges the President to support humanitarian activities taking place in and outside Syria, support efforts for a peaceful resolution of the conflict in Syria, and contribute to future capacity building for governing institutions after a political transition takes place in Syria.

Directs the President to instruct the Secretary of State to work with the appropriate United Nations (U.N.) agencies, regional organizations, nongovernmental organizations, and the broader international community to establish an international donors group to provide humanitarian assistance for the people of Syria.
Document 7: Directs the President to report to Congress outlining a strategy to end U.S. combat operations in Iraq.
Authorizes the President to provide assistance to strengthen democracy, governance, human rights, the rule of law, and religious freedom in Iraq.

topic.name: Elementary and Secondary Education Act Reauthorization
topic.description: The documents focus on the reauthorization and improvement of programs under the Elementary and Secondary Education Act (ESEA) aimed at enhancing educational standards and support for disadvantaged students.

Document 1: This bill amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 (ESEA) to reauthorize through FY2020 the following programs: state assessments; education of migratory children; prevention and intervention for children and youth who are neglected, delinquent, or at-risk; and federal evaluation activities.
The bill consolidates funding for School Improvement Grants, which are aimed at turning around the lowest-performing schools, into the title I-A funding formula.
Document 2: Revises and reauthorizes programs under the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 (ESEA).

Requires states to adopt challenging academic content and achievement standards for mathematics, reading or language arts, and science that ensure that all of their public school students graduate from high school fully prepared for postsecondary education or the workforce.
Document 3: Revises subpart 1 (Charter School Program) of part B (Public Charter Schools) of title V (Promoting Informed Parental Choice and Innovative Programs) of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965.
Requires grantees to use at least 7% of grant funds: to provide technical assistance to subgrantees and authorized public chartering agencies.
Document 4: Revises and reauthorizes programs under the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 (ESEA).
Maintains the requirement conditioning a state's receipt of funds under part A on its developing and implementing academic content and achievement standards and assessments.

topic.name: U.S. National Security and Policy
topic.description: Documents focus on U.S. national security policies and strategies addressing global challenges such as climate change, cybersecurity, manufacturing, and interagency cooperation.

Document 1: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to submit to Congress a National Intelligence Estimate on the anticipated geopolitical effects of global climate change and the implications of such effects on U.S. national security.

Expresses the sense of Congress that the Secretary should address the findings of the estimate regarding the impact of global climate change and potential implications of such impact on the Armed Forces.
Document 2: Amends the State Department Basic Authorities Act of 1956 to establish a Coordinator for Cyberspace and Cybersecurity Issues.
Provide strategic direction and coordination for U.S. policy and programs addressing cyberspace and cybersecurity issues overseas.
Document 3: The U.S. government should promote policies related to the nation's manufacturing sector intended to promote growth, sustainability, and competitiveness, create well-paying jobs, enable innovation and investment, and support national security.
Directs the President to establish the President's Manufacturing Strategy Board to advise on issues affecting the nation's manufacturing sector.
Document 4: Directs the President to establish the International Conservation Strategy to help combat global natural resource and biodiversity degradation.

Requires the Director of National Intelligence to submit to Congress a national intelligence estimate on the anticipated geopolitical and regional security effects of the global degradation of renewable natural resources.
Document 5: The U.S. government should promote policies related to the nation's manufacturing sector intended to promote growth, sustainability, and competitiveness, create well-paying jobs, enable innovation and investment, and support national security.
Directs the President to establish the President's Manufacturing Strategy Board to advise on issues affecting the manufacturing sector.
Document 6: Establishes the Interagency Cooperation Commission to examine the long-term global challenges facing the United States.
Develop legislative and administrative proposals to improve interagency cooperation.
Document 7: Establishes in the legislative branch an independent advisory panel to comprehensively assess the management structure and capabilities related to the Department of Homeland Security.
Make recommendations to improve the efficiency and effectiveness of DHS management.

topic.name: Medicare Supervision Requirements
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative amendments related to supervision requirements for healthcare services under the Medicare program.

Document 1: Requires such facilities, instead, to use the services of at least one registered professional nurse to provide assessment, surveillance, and direct care to residents 24 hours a day, seven days a week.
Document 2: if the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) requires supervision by a physician or non-physician practitioner for Medicare payment for therapeutic hospital outpatient services, that requirement is met if such services are furnished under the physician's or non-physician practitioner's general supervision.
Document 3: Medicare reimbursement should be equal for the same service provided to a cancer patient regardless of whether the service is delivered in the hospital outpatient department (OPD) or physician's office.
Document 4: if the Secretary of Health and Human Services requires supervision by a physician or non-physician practitioner for Medicare payment for therapeutic hospital outpatient services, that requirement is met if such services are furnished under the physician's or non-physician practitioner's general supervision.
Document 5: revise physician supervision requirements under the Medicare program for radiology services performed by advanced level radiographers.
Document 6: revise physician supervision requirements under the Medicare program for radiology services performed by advanced level radiographers.
Document 7: if the Secretary of Health and Human Services requires supervision by a physician or non-physician practitioner for Medicare payment for therapeutic hospital outpatient services, that requirement is met if such services are furnished under the physician's or non-physician practitioner's general supervision.

topic.name: Occupational Safety and Health Regulations
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving workplace safety and health protections for employees, including whistleblower protections and reporting requirements.

Document 1: Amends the Occupational Safety and Health Act of 1970 (OSHA-1970) to expand its coverage to federal, state, and local government employees.
Increases protections for whistle blowers under OSHA-1970.
Document 2: Amends the Occupational Safety and Health Act of 1970 (OSHA) to expand its coverage to federal, state, and local government employees.
Prescribes requirements relating to employer reporting of employee work-related deaths or hospitalizations.
Document 3: Increases protections for whistle blowers under OSHA-1970.
Prescribes requirements relating to the posting of employee rights.
Document 4: Declares OSHA inapplicable to working conditions covered by the Federal Mine Safety and Health Act of 1977.
A prohibition against employers adopting or implementing policies or practices that discourage or discriminate against employee reporting of work-related injuries or illnesses.
Document 5: Authorizes the Secretary of Labor, under specified conditions, to cede OSHA jurisdiction to another federal agency.
Investigations of incidents resulting in death or the hospitalization of two or more employees which occur in a place of employment.
Document 6: A site-controlling employer's duty to keep a site log for recordable injuries and illnesses of all employees.
A prohibition against the issuing, modifying, or settling of unclassified citations for occupational health and safety standard violations.
Document 7: Prohibits covered entities from engaging in employment discrimination on the basis of an individual's actual or perceived sexual orientation or gender identity.
Specifies that such unlawful employment practices include actions based on the actual or perceived orientation or identity of a person with whom the individual associates.

topic.name: Aviation Funding and Modernization
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at authorizing funding and improvements for the Federal Aviation Administration and airport infrastructure.

Document 1: Amends federal transportation law to authorize appropriations for FY2008-FY2011 for FAA operations
Directs the FAA to impose a surcharge of $25 per flight on owners or operators of passenger aircraft
Document 2: Reauthorizes appropriations for FY2008-FY2011 for airport planning and development
Amends the airport improvement program to increase passenger facility charge amounts
Document 3: Authorizes appropriations to the FAA for FY2010 and FY2011 for operations
Directs the FAA Administrator to establish a program to utilize undergraduate and technical colleges
Document 5: Reauthorizes appropriations to the FAA for FY2010-FY2012 for airport planning and development
Amends the airport improvement program to increase passenger facility charge amounts
Document 6: Authorizes appropriations to the Secretary of Transportation for FY2012-FY2015 for airport planning and development
Requires the FAA Administrator to include in the Airway Capital Investment Plan a list of capital projects

topic.name: Land Management and Conservation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative acts focused on the management, protection, and conservation of federal lands and natural resources, particularly in wilderness and special management areas.

Document 1: Designates approximately 26,000 acres of land managed by the Bureau of Land Management (BLM) in Humboldt County, Nevada, as the Pine Forest Range Wilderness.

Withdraws the Wilderness from: (1) entry, appropriation, and disposal under the public land laws; (2) location, entry, and patent under the mining laws; and (3) disposition under laws relating to mineral and geothermal leasing or mineral materials.
Document 2: Permits specified federal lands in San Juan National Forest in Colorado to be called the Hermosa Creek Watershed and Protection Area.

Withdraws federal land within the Special Management Area from: (1) entry, appropriation, or disposal under public land laws; (2) location, entry, and patent under mining laws; and (3) disposition under mineral leasing, geothermal leasing, or mineral materials laws.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to issue a special use authorization to the owners of a water storage, transport, or diversion facility located on National Forest System land in the Frank Church-River of No Return Wilderness.

Requires the Secretary, in a special use authorization under this Act, to: (1) allow the use of motorized equipment and mechanized transport for operation, maintenance, or reconstruction of a facility.

topic.name: Child Immigration and Protection Policies
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at the immigration status and protection of children, particularly unaccompanied minors and adopted children, within the United States.

Document 1: Protection of Children Act of 2015 amends the William Wilberforce Trafficking Victims Protection Reauthorization Act of 2008
An immigration officer who finds an inadmissible UAC at a land border or port of entry shall return the child to his or her country of nationality.
Document 2: Bar Removal of Individuals who Dream and Grow our Economy Act or the BRIDGE Act provides provisional protected presence to qualifying aliens.
An alien is eligible for such protected presence and employment authorization if the alien was born after June 15, 1981.
Document 3: Recognizing American Children Act authorizes the Department of Homeland Security to cancel the removal of, and adjust to conditional nonimmigrant for an initial five-year period the status of, an alien who was younger than 16 years old when he or she initially entered the United States.
Conditional nonimmigrant status shall include employment and military enlistment authorization.
Document 5: Protection of Children Act of 2016 amends the William Wilberforce Trafficking Victims Protection Reauthorization Act of 2008
An unaccompanied child who is not a victim of a severe form of trafficking in persons... shall have a hearing before an immigration judge within 14 days.
Document 6: Foreign Adopted Children Equality Act sets forth automatic citizenship criteria for a child adopted outside the United States by a U.S. citizen parent.
Deems such an adopted child to be a U.S. citizen at birth.
Document 7: Foreign Adopted Children Equality Act sets forth automatic citizenship criteria for a child adopted outside the United States by a U.S. citizen parent.
Deems such an adopted child to be a U.S. citizen at birth.

topic.name: Support for Beginning Farmers
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at assisting beginning and disadvantaged farmers through various programs and grants.

Document 1: Amends the Food Security Act of 1985 to extend assistance to disadvantaged or beginning farmers or ranchers for access to the environmental quality incentives and the conservation stewardship programs.
Provides priority assistance for new farming opportunities, including young, beginning, socially disadvantaged, and immigrant issues.
Document 2: Provides for the inclusion of locally or regionally produced agricultural food products under specified agricultural, rural development, agricultural research, crop insurance, and nutrition programs.
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to carry out the Green and Healthy Corner Store Initiative to assist qualified convenience stores to expand their offering of fruits and vegetables.
Directs the Secretary to make grants to provide access to farmers' markets for communities with limited access to affordable and nutritious food.

topic.name: Public Health Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving public health through research, education, and disease prevention initiatives.

Document 1: Pulmonary Fibrosis Research Enhancement Act - Amends the Public Health Service Act to require the Director of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) to establish the National Pulmonary Fibrosis Advisory Board.
Requires the Secretary to ensure that epidemiological and other types of information are made available to the National Institutes of Health (NIH) and the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA).
Document 2: Lung Cancer Mortality Reduction Act of 2009 - Amends the Public Health Service Act to require the Secretary of Health and Human Services to implement a comprehensive program to achieve a 50% reduction in the mortality rate of lung cancer.
Includes within such program: a strategic review and prioritization of research grants.
Document 3: Lyme and Tick-Borne Diseases Prevention, Education, and Research Act of 2013 - Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to establish the Tick-Borne Diseases Advisory Committee.
Requires the Secretary to provide for the coordination of all federal programs and activities related to Lyme and other tick-borne diseases.
Document 4: Pancreatic Cancer Research and Education Act - Amends the Public Health Service Act to require the Secretary of Health and Human Services to establish and implement a Pancreatic Cancer Initiative.
Requires the Director of NIH and the Director of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) to develop a communication tool kit for patients and their families.
Document 5: Lyme and Tick-Borne Disease Prevention, Education, and Research Act of 2013 - Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to establish the Tick-Borne Diseases Advisory Committee.
Directs the Committee to regularly review published public and private treatment guidelines.
Document 6: Gestational Diabetes Act of 2010 or the GEDI Act - Amends the Public Health Service Act to direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services to develop a multisite gestational diabetes research project.
Requires the Secretary to expand and intensify public health research on gestational diabetes.
Document 7: Blueprint for Health Act of 2009 - Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to create the Blueprint for Health program.
Sets forth elements to be included in the Blueprint, including a strategic plan for designing an integrated medical home model of care.

topic.name: Consumer Protection Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing consumer rights and safety in various markets.

Document 1: Thrift Store Protection Act - Allows secondhand resellers to sell specified used products treated as banned hazardous substances.
Defines 'secondhand seller' as a consignment shop, thrift shop, or similar enterprise.
Document 2: Video Game Rating Enforcement Act of 2008 - Prohibits shipping or distributing video games without an age-based content rating.
Treats violations as an unfair or deceptive act subject to civil penalties.
Document 3: Transparent Baseball Ticket Fees Act - Makes it unlawful to sell tickets without disclosing associated fees.
Treats such offense as an unfair or deceptive act under the Federal Trade Commission Act.
Document 4: Cell Phone Theft Prevention Act of 2013 - Prohibits service on stolen mobile devices and requires remote deletion capability.
Establishes criminal penalties for altering mobile device identification numbers.
Document 5: Cloned Food Labeling Act - Deems food containing cloned animal parts as misbranded without proper labeling.
Sets forth civil penalties for violation of this Act.
Document 6: Cloned Food Labeling Act - Similar provisions as Document 5, emphasizing the need for proper labeling and recordkeeping.
Document 7: Consumer Right to Know Food Labeling Act of 2010 - Similar provisions as Document 5, ensuring transparency in food labeling.

topic.name: Guantanamo Bay Detention Policies

unless the President certifies to Congress that...

Document 6: This bill prohibits the use of funds to transfer, release, or assist in the transfer or release...

topic.name: Oil and Gas Leasing Regulations
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts and amendments related to the regulation and administration of oil and gas leasing on federal lands and the Outer Continental Shelf.

Document 1: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act (OCSLA) with respect to Adjacent Zones and Planning Areas in the outer Continental Shelf (OCS) subsoil and seabed.
Establishes the Deficit Reduction Trust Fund to accept specified OCS receipts to reduce the federal debt.
Document 2: Amends the Mineral Leasing Act to repeal the requirement that leases be issued within 60 days following payment.
Directs the Secretary to automatically issue a lease 60 days after the date of such payment.
Document 3: Deems the Draft Proposed Outer Continental Shelf Oil and Gas Leasing Program 2010-2015 to have been approved by the Secretary of the Interior.
Directs the Secretary to conduct a lease sale every 270 days in each outer Continental Shelf (OCS) planning region.
Document 4: Authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to grant a right of way for natural gas pipelines through a unit of the National Park System.
Defines 'gas gathering line and associated field compression unit' as a pipeline installed to transport natural gas production.
